frame_analyses.description: Title screen - Laparoscopic Hands on Training Institute
Participant seated at training station with endoloop visible on field
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pre-tied ligating loop present, foam appendage visible on field

frame_analyses.description: Right hand grasping endoloop introducer, left hand stabilizing
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper bimanual approach, endoloop in correct orientation

frame_analyses.description: Positioning endoloop near foam appendage target
Advancing loop over foam appendage, attempting to position at marked line
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop being deployed over target, positioning appears accurate

frame_analyses.description: Tightening loop by pulling on suture tail
Continuing to tighten loop around appendage
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Progressive tightening, loop appears to be at marked position

frame_analyses.description: Using scissors to cut excess suture tail
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cutting motion, appropriate length maintained

frame_analyses.description: Final trimming of suture tail near cinched loop
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion, loop appears secure and properly positioned

penalties.description: Loop appears to be approximately 2-3mm distal to the marked line

confidence_rationale: Loop positioning relative to mark is somewhat difficult to assess precisely due to camera angle and lighting. Completion time and cinching quality are clearly observable. No major penalties like transection clearly visible.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation from mark - camera angle makes precise measurement challenging
Whether any minor loosening occurred during final phases
technique_summary: Intermediate to novice performance with extended time in cinching and cutting phases. Loop deployment and approach phases were appropriately paced. Primary time loss occurred in the final securing and trimming phases.
strengths: No appendage transection or major complications
improvement_suggestions: Reduce time spent in cinching phase from 35s to under 20s
More efficient suture tail cutting - reduce from 60s to under 15s
Practice precise loop positioning to minimize deviation from marked line
